subject: English
skill: Ask and Answer Questions About Text

lesson.story_mission: Join the Evidence Explorers and answer questions only when the passage proves your answer.
lesson.objective: I can answer who, what, where, when, why, and how questions using text evidence.
lesson.concept_explanation: Question words tell readers what kind of answer to find. Strong readers return to the passage and use details as evidence.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may answer from memory or a picture instead of checking the words in the passage.
lesson.real_world_connection: Using evidence helps readers explain answers in class discussions, book talks, and research.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the question word, reread the passage, find evidence, then answer in your own words.
lesson.mini_lesson: Ask what the question word is looking for, then prove the answer with a sentence from the text.

worked_examples.text: Watch: The question asks who, so reread to find the person named in the passage.
worked_examples.passage: Nina carried a tray of bean seedlings to the school garden on Friday morning. Her class planted them beside the fence because the spot was sunny. Nina watered each plant so the roots would not dry out.
worked_examples.evidence_span: Nina carried a tray of bean seedlings to the school garden on Friday morning.

worked_examples.text: Demo: The sentence with because often helps answer why.
worked_examples.passage: Nina carried a tray of bean seedlings to the school garden on Friday morning. Her class planted them beside the fence because the spot was sunny. Nina watered each plant so the roots would not dry out.

hint_ladder: Go back to the passage and find the sentence that answers it.
Use words from the text as evidence.

misconception_bank.unsupported_answer: The answer is not supported by the passage.
misconception_bank.question_word_confusion: The answer matches the wrong question word.
misconception_bank.misses_text_evidence: The answer is reasonable but does not cite or use evidence.
misconception_bank.detail_inference_confusion: The answer confuses stated details with an inference.

game_theme.mission_intro: Use passage clues to answer who, what, where, when, why, and how questions.

level_banks.label: Level 1: Who / What / Where
level_banks.focus: Answer who, what, and where questions from short texts.

questions.prompt: Who spotted the frog on the log?

Reread the passage and find the detail that answers it.
Check that the text supports your answer.
questions.explanation: The passage says that Lena spotted the green frog.
questions.feedback: Yes — The passage says that Lena spotted the green frog.

audio.label: Hear the passage

Soon, a round nest rested firmly in the tree.
The tree grew beside a fence.
Next, it pressed soft mud around the grass.
